title: Two consecutive typhoons hit Japan and Taiwan with torrential rains
summary: Typhoons 7 and 8 caused floods, landslides, and evacuation orders in several regions, in addition to causing casualties in Taiwan.

title: Two people drown while practicing SUP in a river in Ehime
summary: The flow of the Kamo River had increased due to the typhoon, dragging three people, of which two died.

title: Magnitude 6.1 earthquake in Yamanashi leaves 21 injured
summary: The quake, caused by the collision of tectonic plates, affected five prefectures and generated concern for Mount Fuji.

title: Chinese and Russian military aircraft conduct a joint flight near Japan
summary: Four bombers traversed the airspace between Okinawa and Shikoku without violating Japanese sovereignty, in a show of cooperation.

title: A small plane crashes into a skyscraper in Beijing: one dead and 13 injured
summary: The pilot died and 13 people were injured; authorities reported the accident a day later.

title: United States and Iran exchange military attacks after signing a memorandum
summary: Hostilities resumed with attacks on positions in Bahrain and the Strait of Hormuz, jeopardizing the truce.

title: Cape Verde advances to the Round of 16 of the World Cup for the first time
summary: The African team drew goalless against Saudi Arabia and qualified as one of the best third-placed teams in their World Cup debut.

title: US government authorizes limited resumption of the 'Mythos' AI model
summary: Access to the advanced artificial intelligence system is restricted to US companies and excludes foreign entities.

title: The Japanese national soccer team faces Brazil in an early morning friendly
summary: The match will serve to gauge the level of Japanese soccer against the South American powerhouse, with age restrictions on broadcasts.

title: Japanese writer Shōji Kaoru, author of 'Beware, Little Red Riding Hood', dies
summary: The novelist, 88, was known for his work that portrayed the youth of the 1960s.

title: Mayor elected by one vote challenges annulment of his victory in Tochigi
summary: The mayor of Nasu files a lawsuit to overturn the decision that annulled his election by a margin of a single vote.

title: Veteran pitcher Takayuki Kishi leads the Rakuten's winning streak
summary: At 41, Kishi shows masterful control and is grateful to continue competing in professional baseball.

title: A landslide in Yamaguchi causes the death of an elderly man
summary: Heavy rains caused a landslide that buried a home in Hirao, leaving one dead and three injured.

title: Japan New Drug qualifies for the national corporate baseball tournament
summary: The pharmaceutical company team secures its place at the Dome Stadium after four years of absence.

title: New president of Kajima Construction promises to improve salaries and value workers
summary: Kiryu assumes leadership of the construction company emphasizing the integration of the value chain and treatment of employees.

title: Former leader of South Korean conservative party debates party rebuilding
summary: Han Dong-hoon gives an interview about the future of conservatism in South Korea after the recent electoral defeat.

title: 32 years since the Matsumoto sarin gas attack: tribute and debate on privacy
summary: The community remembers the victims while a lawyer criticizes the publication of the names of the suspects.

title: Japan faces the challenge of integrating foreign workers amid discrimination
summary: A teacher of Brazilian descent advocates for inclusion, while barriers for immigrants persist.

title: Daily and weekly horoscope for late June
summary: Astrological predictions for the zodiac signs, with advice for love, work, and shopping.

title: Japan loses to New Zealand Māori in rugby preparation match
summary: The Japanese team showed its youth and learned valuable lessons against a physically superior team.
